sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: CVB Financial Corp. Reports Q4 2025 Net Earnings of $55 Million, EPS of $0.40
feed.summary: CVB Financial Corp. announced strong financial results for Q4 2025, with net earnings of $55 million, a 4.1% increase from the previous quarter, and an annual net income of $209.3 million, up 4.3% from 2024. The company also reported an expanded net interest margin of 3.49% and significant loan and deposit growth. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for CVBF, with an average 1-year price target of $23.83.

feed.title: Mark Pearson Sells 39,700 Shares of Equitable (NYSE:EQH) Stock
feed.summary: Equitable Holdings CEO Mark Pearson sold 39,700 shares of company stock for approximately $1.83 million on January 20, reducing his ownership by 5.73%. This sale follows earlier transactions in December and November. The company recently missed earnings estimates, reporting $1.48 EPS against an expected $1.59, and revenue of $1.45 billion against $3.65 billion.

feed.title: Caleres Announces CFO Transition
feed.summary: Caleres (NYSE: CAL) announced that Dan Karpel, the company’s senior vice president and chief accounting officer, has been appointed interim chief financial officer, effective immediately. He succeeds Jack Calandra, who is leaving at the end of the month. The company also provided an updated Q4 outlook, noting potential impacts from the Saks Global bankruptcy and restructuring charges, which could affect sales and earnings per diluted share guidance.

feed.title: Braskem Discloses Individual Investor’s 5% Stake in Class B Preferred Shares
feed.summary: Braskem SA announced that individual investor Victor Adler acquired 24,000 class B preferred shares, constituting a 5.013% stake in the company. Adler's acquisition is for investment purposes and is not intended to change corporate control or governance. Currently, the analyst rating for BAK stock is a Hold with a $3.30 price target, while TipRanks' AI Analyst Spark views BAK as a Neutral due to financial challenges despite some positive indicators.

feed.title: Astronics Corporation stock hits all-time high at 75.81 USD
feed.summary: Astronics Corporation (ATRO) stock has reached an all-time high of $75.81, reflecting a 341.35% increase over the past year and 114.36% in the last six months, pushing its market cap to $2.69 billion. Despite the stock being in overbought territory according to its RSI, analysts maintain a "Buy" recommendation and expect the company to return to profitability this year. Recent positive developments include surpassing Q3 2025 earnings expectations, upgraded price targets from Craig-Hallum, and new "Buy" coverage from TD Cowen.

feed.title: Ex-Unilever CEO Schumacher named Barry Callebaut CEO
feed.summary: Barry Callebaut has appointed Hein Schumacher, former CEO of Unilever and FrieslandCampina, as its new chief executive. Schumacher, who previously led Unilever when it decided to spin off its ice-cream business, replaces Peter Feld, who is departing to pursue other career opportunities. The announcement coincides with Barry Callebaut's Q1 financial results, showing an 8.9% revenue increase but a nearly 10% decline in volumes.

feed.title: CompoSecure, a Reporting Segment of GPGI, Inc, Announces CEO Transition
feed.summary: CompoSecure, a reporting segment of GPGI, Inc., has appointed Graham Robinson as its new President and Chief Executive Officer, effective January 22, 2026. Robinson, an industry veteran with 30 years of experience, will lead CompoSecure's next phase of growth following CEO Jon Wilk's 10 years of service. This transition comes as CompoSecure, Inc. rebrands to GPGI, Inc. and has recently completed a business combination with Husky Technologies, creating a diversified multi-industry company focusing on recurring revenues, high margins, and robust free cash flow.

feed.title: AMERICAN REBEL HOLIDNGS, INC. (NASDAQ: AREB) ANNOUNCES
feed.summary: American Rebel Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: AREB) announced a 1-for-20 reverse stock split with round lot shareholder protection, effective February 2, 2026. This move aims to increase the per-share price for Nasdaq compliance and enhance marketability, without affecting the total investment value. Fractional shares will be rounded up, and shareholders holding 100 or more pre-split shares will retain at least 100 post-split shares.

feed.title: Automatic Data Processing Expects $21.8 Billion Revenue, Continues 51-Year Dividend Growth
feed.summary: Automatic Data Processing (ADP) expects to achieve $21.8 billion in revenue for the fiscal year, marking a 5.8% increase, driven by its strong market presence in payroll processing and tax reporting. The company boasts a 51-year streak of dividend increases, reflecting its stable profitability and a forward dividend yield of 2.6%. While analyst opinions vary, with some downgrades due to potential headwinds, the consensus one-year price target is $290.27, suggesting growth potential.

feed.title: MITSUI SUMITOMO INSURANCE CO LTD Increases Stake in WR Berkley C
feed.summary: MITSUI SUMITOMO INSURANCE CO LTD, a 10% owner of WR Berkley Corp, recently purchased an additional 630,000 shares, bringing their total holdings to over 53 million shares. This transaction is part of a larger trend of insider buying at WR Berkley Corp over the past year, with eight insider purchases and no sales, indicating positive sentiment. The stock is currently deemed fairly valued based on its GF Value, with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 1.01.

feed.title: Verisign Stock: Quiet Domain Giant, Loud Numbers – Is Wall Street Underestimating This Cash Machin
feed.summary: Verisign, the internet's core domain registry for .com and .net, is highlighted as a steady, cash-generating stock often overlooked by investors focused on flashier tech. Despite not being a hyper-growth company, its consistent free cash flow, share buybacks, and reliable revenue from domain renewals and moderate price increases make it an attractive long-term investment. Wall Street analysts generally rate it a "Hold" or "Market Perform," valuing its predictable cash flows and essential role in the internet's infrastructure.

feed.title: Harry N. Vafias-led C3is buys South Korean product tanker for $22.9m
feed.summary: C3is Inc., led by Harry N. Vafias, has acquired a 2011-built South Korean product tanker with 47,203 dwt capacity for $22.9 million. The transaction, approved by independent directors and involving an entity affiliated with the Vafias family, allows C3is to pay for the vessel one year after the purchase agreement without interest. This acquisition will increase C3is's fleet to six vessels, substantially enhancing its overall capacity.

feed.title: Amylyx Pharmaceuticals stock hits 52-week high at $16.96 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Amylyx Pharmaceuticals (AMLX) reached a 52-week high of $16.96, reflecting a 380.55% increase over the past year. This growth highlights strong investor confidence in the company, which continues to expand and innovate within the pharmaceutical industry despite being unprofitable in the last twelve months. The company recently announced the selection of AMX0318 for post-bariatric hypoglycemia and rare diseases, prompting Mizuho to reiterate an "Outperform" rating with an $18.00 price target.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Maintains PG&E Corp(PCG.US) With Hold Rating, Raises Target Price to $21
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley analyst David Arcaro has maintained a "Hold" rating on PG&E Corp (PCG.US) and increased the target price from $20 to $21. According to TipRanks data, Arcaro has a 61.8% success rate and an average return of 13.1% over the past year. TipRanks provides independent analysis of financial analysts' recommendations, including success rates and average returns, to help inform investment decisions.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) Expected to Beat Earnings Estimates: Can the Stock Move Higher?
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) is expected to report a year-over-year decline in earnings and revenues for the quarter ended December 2025, with results anticipated on January 28. However, the company has a positive Earnings ESP of +0.33% and a Zacks Rank #3, suggesting a strong likelihood of an earnings beat. This combination, along with a history of beating EPS estimates, indicates that the stock may move higher following the report.

feed.title: Banco Bilbao Viscaya Argentaria (NYSE:BBVA) Sets New 1-Year High - Time to Buy?
feed.summary: Banco Bilbao Viscaya Argentaria (NYSE:BBVA) reached a new 52-week high of $24.84, trading significantly above its 50-day and 200-day moving averages, indicating strong upward momentum. While analyst sentiment is mixed with recent downgrades to "hold" from some firms, the MarketBeat consensus remains a "Moderate Buy." The bank exhibits strong fundamentals, including a market cap of approximately $143 billion, a P/E of 12.55, a PEG of 0.96, and profitability metrics like a 17.07% ROE and 28.58% net margin, having beaten its last quarterly EPS estimate.

feed.title: City Holding (CHCO) Margin Strength Reinforces Bullish Narratives Despite Modest Growth Expectations
feed.summary: City Holding (CHCO) reported strong FY 2025 results with a 12.5% earnings growth and a 41.3% net profit margin, despite modest revenue growth expectations of 3.1%. While bullish narratives are supported by consistent profit growth and a stable loan book, cautious investors note the conflict between current strong margins and projected earnings decline, alongside a P/E ratio above peers. The stock trades below its DCF fair value, offering a mixed valuation picture.

feed.title: Assessing S&P Global (SPGI) Valuation After Strong Multi‑Year Shareholder Returns
feed.summary: S&P Global (SPGI) has seen strong multi-year shareholder returns, but its current valuation suggests it may be overvalued. Trading at a Price-to-Earnings ratio of 38.1x, it is significantly higher than industry and peer averages, and above its estimated fair value based on discounted cash flow. While the company exhibits healthy revenue and net income growth, investors are paying a premium for current earnings.

feed.title: Boeing starts hiring for delayed 737 line in Everett, the city welcomes economic impacts
feed.summary: Boeing has begun hiring for its new North Line in Everett for 737 production, a move welcomed by local officials. The new hires will gain experience on certified 737 MAX 8 and 9 models while awaiting FAA certification for the 737 MAX 10, addressing previous delays and safety concerns that have plagued the company. This expansion is expected to bring significant economic benefits to Everett, a city heavily reliant on Boeing's presence.

feed.title: Entergy Corp: Defensive Dividend Player Tests Investor Nerves As Stock Pulls Back From 52?Week High
feed.summary: Entergy Corp's stock has recently pulled back from a multi-month rally, prompting investors to assess whether this is a temporary consolidation or a deeper shift in market sentiment. Despite the recent dip, the defensive utility stock has delivered a respectable 8% price gain over the past year, exceeding 12% total return including dividends. Wall Street analysts remain cautiously constructive, with most ratings leaning toward Buy or Hold, citing stable regulated earnings and capital investment plans, while cautioning about interest rate sensitivity.

feed.title: ConocoPhillips Downgrade Weighs Breakeven Costs Against Valuation And Venezuela Risks
feed.summary: BofA Securities downgraded ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) due to elevated breakeven costs compared to its peers, concerns over long-cycle projects with delayed production, and the ongoing legal and political dispute with Venezuela. These factors introduce uncertainty regarding future cash flows and capital allocation for the independent oil and gas producer. Despite the downgrade, Simply Wall St's assessment suggests ConocoPhillips shares might be undervalued relative to analyst targets and estimated fair value, with recent positive price momentum.

feed.title: Xenia Hotels & Resorts Stock: Solid Fundamentals And Rising Dividends (NYSE:XHR)
feed.summary: Xenia Hotels & Resorts (XHR) has been upgraded to 'Buy' due to its undervalued shares and strong balance sheet. The company's diverse portfolio and focus on business and group segments provide stability, while management's preference for share buybacks indicates confidence in its intrinsic value, projecting a potential 13% upside. With a low dividend payout ratio, future dividend increases are anticipated, enhancing yield prospects.

feed.title: Martin Small Sells 27,047 Shares of BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) Stock
feed.summary: BlackRock CFO Martin Small sold 27,047 shares of BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) stock on January 16th, generating over $31.68 million and reducing his ownership by 71.93%. This sale follows BlackRock's strong quarterly earnings, which surpassed analyst expectations, and an announced increase in its quarterly dividend. Analysts currently rate BlackRock as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus target price of $1,314.71.

feed.title: Holdings Venture Globalharvest Purchases 300,000 Shares of Mission Produce (NASDAQ:AVO) Stock
feed.summary: Holdings Venture Globalharvest recently purchased 300,000 shares of Mission Produce (NASDAQ:AVO) for $3.75 million on January 20th, increasing its direct stake to over 9.46 million shares. This latest transaction follows several other significant purchases made by the firm throughout January and late December. Mission Produce's stock reacted positively, rising 4.5%, despite mixed financial results where quarterly EPS missed estimates but revenue exceeded them.

feed.title: Ingersoll Rand (NYSE:IR) Insider Sells $16,830,920.00 in Stock
feed.summary: Ingersoll Rand (NYSE:IR) director Vicente Reynal sold 191,000 shares of the company's stock for a total of $16,830,920.00 on January 16, 2026, reducing his stake by nearly 50%. The stock subsequently rose 5.4% to $89.79, and the company has a market capitalization of $35.48 billion. Analysts currently have an average "Hold" rating with a target price of $91.38 for IR.

feed.title: Eli Lilly and Company (NYSE:LLY) Stock Price Up 3.6% - Time to Buy?
feed.summary: Eli Lilly and Company's stock price increased by 3.6% on Wednesday to $1,078.9780, driven by positive regulatory and clinical news, including Breakthrough Therapy designation for sofetabart and progress in its obesity and pain pipelines. Analysts maintain a bullish outlook, with a consensus "Buy" rating and an average price target of $1,174.61, following a strong Q4 beat and elevated dividend. The company's diverse pipeline and GLP-1 leadership are key growth drivers, despite competitive pressures and minor analyst target adjustments.

feed.title: Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) Trading 2.6% Higher - Time to Buy?
feed.summary: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) saw its stock price rise 2.6% to $32.24 on heavy volume, driven by AI demand, an $11B+ backlog, a new $2B credit facility, and potential short-squeeze activity. Despite positive sentiment from some analysts highlighting its undervaluation and growth avenues, the company faces skepticism due to a recent earnings miss, compressed margins, competition, and analyst downgrades leading to a consensus "Hold" rating with an average target price of $46.19. Institutional investors hold a significant portion of the stock.

feed.title: Ford recalls some 119,000 vehicles due to engine block heater fire risk, NHTSA says
feed.summary: Ford is recalling approximately 119,000 vehicles due to a defect in their engine block heaters that poses a fire risk when plugged in. The affected models include various Focus, Explorer, Escape, and MKC vehicles from different years equipped with 2.0L engines. Owners are advised to immediately stop using the engine block heaters, and Ford is developing a new heater element for free replacement, with an option for removal and a threaded plug installation.

feed.title: Verizon Stock Edges Higher As Yield Hunters Circle: Is The Worst Finally Over For VZ?
feed.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. has seen a quiet recovery in its stock price, attracting yield-focused investors with its dividend and improving balance sheet. Despite past concerns over debt and competition, recent quarterly results showing stabilizing subscriber trends and free cash flow have eased fears of a dividend cut. Wall Street analysts have largely maintained constructive but moderate ratings, suggesting Verizon is now a suitable holding for diversified portfolios with steady, rather than explosive, growth expectations.

feed.title: The Truth About Westwater Resources (WWR): Tiny Stock, Massive Hype – Is It About To Explode Or Fa
feed.summary: Westwater Resources (WWR) is gaining attention as a US-based graphite and battery-materials company, vital for EVs and energy storage. The article discusses the high-risk, high-reward nature of this small-cap stock, emphasizing its dependency on financing and its volatile price performance driven by hype and news. Investors are advised to view WWR as a speculative play given its early development stage and potential for significant fluctuations.

feed.title: The Truth About Equity Residential: Is This Apartment Giant a Sleeper Money Machine or Total Flop?
feed.summary: This article evaluates Equity Residential (EQR), an apartment REIT, as a potential investment. It analyzes EQR's business model, stock performance, market sentiment, risk factors, and competitive landscape, concluding it's a solid "cop" for long-term, dividend-focused investors seeking real estate exposure, despite lacking the viral appeal of meme stocks.

feed.title: Intel, Moderna, TMC, Microsoft And Lucid: Why These 5 Stocks Are On Investors' Radars Today
feed.summary: The stock market saw a rally today following positive international trade news, specifically President Trump's suspension of tariffs on EU countries. Intel, Moderna, The Metals Company, Microsoft, and Lucid Group were key stocks attracting investor attention. Intel is anticipated to release Q4 earnings, Moderna's cancer therapy study results boosted its shares, TMC benefited from new deep seabed mining regulations, Microsoft's CEO discussed AI's tangible benefits, and Lucid announced an expanded manufacturing partnership in Saudi Arabia.

feed.title: Manitoba John Deere dealers plan merger
feed.summary: Greenvalley Equipment and Enns Brothers, two John Deere dealerships in Manitoba, announced their intent to merge their operations. This consolidation aims to improve parts access, service speed, and long-term support for farmers by combining resources, expertise, and technology. The merger, expected to be completed in April 2026, will create a larger dealership network while maintaining local ownership and existing staff.

feed.title: Trump Administration Launches Power Auction Initiative, Benefiting GE Vernova
feed.summary: The Trump administration has introduced a power auction initiative with bipartisan support to stabilize residential utility prices by requiring tech giants to fund new power plants. This policy is expected to significantly benefit GE Vernova, due to its role as a key supplier of power generation equipment, leading to a 77% stock surge over the past year and substantial order growth in its gas power segment. Analysts maintain a generally positive outlook on GE's stock, though some have noted potential oversupply concerns.

feed.title: KFC and Taco Bell parent sets Feb. 4 call on 62,000 restaurants
feed.summary: Yum! Brands, Inc. (NYSE: YUM) is set to release its fourth-quarter 2025 financial results on Wednesday, February 4, 2026, followed by a conference call at 8:15 a.m. ET to discuss its financial performance and strategies. The event will include a live webcast and a Q&A session limited to analysts, with playback available until February 11, 2026. Yum! Brands operates over 62,000 restaurants across more than 155 countries with brands like KFC, Taco Bell, Pizza Hut, and Habit Burger & Grill.

feed.title: Exclusive: Valero buys Venezuelan oil cargo as part of Washington's deal with Caracas
feed.summary: Valero (VLO.N) has purchased a cargo of Venezuelan crude oil, marking the first such deal by a U.S. Gulf Coast refiner under Washington's agreement with Caracas to buy up to 50 million barrels of crude. The crude was bought from trading house Vitol at a discount to Brent crude and is the first purchase from trading houses authorized to market Venezuelan crude this month. This move expands beyond Valero's previous dealings solely through Chevron.

feed.title: Three Dividend Kings: ADP, Walmart, and American States Water
feed.summary: This article highlights three Dividend Kings—Automatic Data Processing (ADP), Walmart (WMT), and American States Water (AWR)—as stable investment choices for long-term investors seeking wealth enhancement through dividends. ADP offers dividend growth with projected revenue increases, Walmart shows strong market presence and stock buybacks despite a lower yield, and American States Water boasts a 70-year dividend growth streak due to sector resilience. Each company demonstrates strong market positions and consistent cash flow in their respective industries, making them suitable for portfolio diversification.

feed.title: Yum! Brands Announces Q4 2025 Earnings and Conference Call Details
feed.summary: Yum! Brands, Inc. (NYSE: YUM) will release its fourth quarter 2025 financial results on Wednesday, February 4, 2026, at 7:00 a.m. ET, followed by a conference call at 8:15 a.m. ET to discuss performance and strategies. The event will be webcast live on the company's website, with dial-in options for analysts and media. Playback will be available from February 4 to February 11, 2026.

feed.title: IBM (IBM) Valuation Check After New AI Products And Partnerships Draw Fresh Attention
feed.summary: IBM has recently introduced new AI products and partnerships, such as GRAMMY IQ powered by watsonx and an agreement with e&, drawing renewed interest in the stock. Despite a P/E ratio of 35.1x, which is higher than its industry and peer averages, Simply Wall St's fair P/E estimate of 38.1x suggests the valuation might be about right. However, their discounted cash flow (DCF) model indicates IBM could be overvalued at its current share price of US$297.54, implying investors are paying above the modelled cash flow value.

feed.title: The Bull Case For Pfizer (PFE) Could Change Following Novavax Matrix-M Adjuvant Licensing Deal - Learn Why
feed.summary: Pfizer recently secured a licensing deal with Novavax for its Matrix-M vaccine adjuvant technology, aiming to enhance future vaccines. This deal is seen as an incremental broadening of Pfizer's vaccine toolkit rather than a transformative event, as the company still faces challenges with rebuilding earnings post-pandemic, managing high debt, and addressing patent expirations. Investors are weighing Pfizer's low valuation against dividend strain and pipeline execution risks, with a wide range of fair value estimates.

feed.title: Assessing DXC Technology (DXC) Valuation As Shares Trade On A 7x P/E And Large DCF-Implied Discount
feed.summary: DXC Technology (DXC) is trading at a P/E ratio of 7x, significantly lower than its peers and the US IT industry average, indicating it may be undervalued. Despite recent share price declines over the past year, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model estimates a fair value of US$29.26 per share, suggesting a large discount compared to its current price of US$14.98. However, weak annual revenue and net income trends, along with multi-year total return declines, present key risks to its operational outlook and investor confidence.

feed.title: Whirlpool to brief investors on results in Jan. 29 call
feed.summary: Whirlpool Corporation (NYSE: WHR) announced it will release its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results on January 28, 2026, at 4:05 p.m. ET. The company will also host an investor conference call to discuss these results on January 29, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. ET. Details for accessing the call and webcast have been provided, with materials available on their investor website.

feed.title: ADTRAN Holdings (ADTN) Projects Q4 2025 Revenue of $293M, Exceeding Guidance
feed.summary: ADTRAN Holdings (ADTN) anticipates fourth-quarter 2025 revenue to be between $290 million and $293 million, surpassing previous guidance and indicating strong demand. The company also projects GAAP operating margin of 1.4% to 1.7% and non-GAAP operating margin of 6.0% to 6.9% for Q4, alongside a full-year 2025 GAAP revenue outlook of $1.082 billion to $1.085 billion. CEO Tom Stanton attributes these preliminary results to increased demand and strong execution, which are expected to boost financial performance and investor confidence.

feed.title: ADTRAN tops revenue targets, says Q4 earnings to beat forecasts
feed.summary: ADTRAN Holdings announced preliminary unaudited Q4 2025 results, reporting revenue guidance exceeding previous forecasts with a range of $290.0 million to $293.0 million. The company also expects its non-GAAP earnings per share for the fourth quarter to surpass current analyst consensus estimates. Final financial results for Q4 and full-year 2025 are scheduled to be released on February 25, 2026.

feed.title: Arrow Electronics Again Named to Fortune’s “World’s Most Admired Companies” List
feed.summary: Arrow Electronics has been recognized for the 26th time on Fortune's "World's Most Admired Companies" list. The company ranked among the top in the "Wholesalers: Electronics and Office Equipment" category, acknowledged for its strong financial performance and high-quality products and services. This prestigious list is compiled based on feedback from thousands of executives, directors, and financial community members.

feed.title: Telix delivers on 2025 revenue targets as Gozellix fuels next phase of growth
feed.summary: Telix Pharmaceuticals (ASX:TLX) has met its upgraded revenue guidance for the 2025 financial year, reporting unaudited group revenue of approximately US$804 million. This success is driven by strong fourth-quarter performance, particularly from its Precision Medicine division and the successful U.S. launch of Gozellix. The company is poised for continued growth into 2026, advancing its therapeutics pipeline and strengthening strategic partnerships.

feed.title: ALX Oncology increases shares reserved for inducement equity plan By Investing.com
feed.summary: ALX Oncology Holdings Inc. has amended its 2025 Inducement Equity Incentive Plan, adding 1,300,000 shares to the reservation, bringing the total to 2,800,000. This amendment, allowed by Nasdaq rules without stockholder approval, enables the company to grant equity awards to new or rejoining employees as an inducement to employment. In other news, ALX Oncology has shown significant progress in its cancer treatment pipeline, particularly with its drug evorpacept, and Jefferies has maintained a Buy rating on the company.

feed.title: Crown Castle (CCI) Announces Year-End Tax Reporting for 2025 Distributions, Cash Dividend of $1.56
feed.summary: Crown Castle (CCI) has announced a cash distribution of $1.56 per share for 2025, with an ordinary taxable dividend of $1.29 per share. The dividends are scheduled for quarterly payments throughout 2025, and the company advises shareholders to consult tax advisors for specific tax treatment. This announcement reinforces Crown Castle's position in communications infrastructure, operating over 40,000 cell towers and 90,000 route miles of fiber.

feed.title: Crown Castle Announces Tax Reporting Information for 2025 Distributions
feed.summary: Crown Castle Inc. has released its year-end tax reporting information for distributions paid to shareholders in 2025. The announcement provides a detailed summary of the cash distributions, ordinary taxable dividends, qualified taxable dividends, and non-taxable distributions on a per-share basis. The company urges shareholders to consult their personal tax advisors regarding the specific tax treatment of these distributions.

feed.title: Capital One (COF) Q4 Earnings Preview: EPS Expected to Rise 34%
feed.summary: Capital One (COF) is set to release its Q4 earnings with an anticipated 34% year-over-year EPS increase to $4.14 and a 51.7% revenue growth to $15.47 billion. The company has a strong record of beating EPS and revenue estimates, and recent analyst revisions show confidence in its future performance, with an average 1-year price target of $284.22.
